trips.name: Amalfi Coast Classic
trips.description: The most celebrated coastal drive in Europe winds between cliff-hanging villages and turquoise sea. From Naples, hug the dramatic SS163 through Positano and Amalfi to the garden city of Ravello.
trips.cover_search_query: Amalfi Coast cliffs Positano Italy

trips.name: Campania Hidden Gems
trips.description: Beyond the famous coast lies a Campania of ancient ruins, baroque hilltowns, and wild Cilento shores. Pompeii anchors history while Paestum's Greek temples stun with their completeness.
trips.cover_search_query: Paestum Greek temples Campania Italy

trips.name: Sorrento Peninsula Loop
trips.description: The Sorrento Peninsula juts between two bays with lemon groves, cliff hotels, and views of Vesuvius. Ferry to Capri or simply loop the headland's serpentine roads.
trips.cover_search_query: Sorrento cliffs Bay of Naples Italy

trips.name: Cilento Wild Coast
trips.description: Cilento is southern Italy's best-kept secret — a wild national park of unspoilt beaches, medieval villages perched on rocky spurs, and crystal-clear coves accessible only by boat.
trips.cover_search_query: Palinuro sea caves Cilento Italy

trips.name: Tuscany Classic
trips.description: Rolling cypress-lined hills, Renaissance cities, and Chianti wine cellars define the essential Tuscan experience. Drive the famous strade bianche between Florence, Siena, and the medieval hilltowns.
trips.cover_search_query: Tuscany cypress hills Val d'Orcia Italy

trips.name: Val d'Orcia & the Crete Senesi
trips.description: UNESCO-listed Val d'Orcia is the landscape that defined Renaissance painting — undulating clay hills, lone farmhouses, and roads lined with ancient cypress trees pointing at the sky.
trips.cover_search_query: Val d'Orcia rolling hills cypress trees Tuscany Italy

trips.name: Maremma & Etruscan Tuscany
trips.description: Wild horses roam the Maremma marshes where the Etruscans once buried their dead in rock-cut tombs. This southwestern Tuscany rewards those who venture beyond the tourist trail.
trips.cover_search_query: Pitigliano tuff rock town Tuscany Italy

trips.name: Lunigiana & Garfagnana Mountains
trips.description: Northern Tuscany hides a mountain world of marble peaks, chestnut forests, and medieval fortresses. The Apuan Alps rear white above the Garfagnana valley, source of the world's finest marble.
trips.cover_search_query: Carrara marble quarries Apuan Alps Italy

trips.name: Chianti Wine Road
trips.description: The Via Chiantigiana threads through the heart of Italy's most famous wine country, passing castle wineries, ancient abbeys, and hilltop villages draped in vines.
trips.cover_search_query: Chianti vineyards wine road Tuscany Italy

trips.name: Tuscan Riviera & Elba
trips.description: Tuscany has a coast too — from chic Forte dei Marmi to the rugged capes of the Argentario and a ferry hop to Napoleon's island of exile, Elba.
trips.cover_search_query: Elba island beach Tuscany Italy

trips.name: Florence & the Renaissance Villages
trips.description: Florence needs no introduction, but the villages that inspired the Renaissance masters are equally revelatory. Fiesole, Impruneta, and Certaldo reward those who leave the city.
trips.cover_search_query: Florence Duomo cathedral Tuscany Italy

trips.name: Sicily Baroque Southeast
trips.description: Rebuilt after a devastating 1693 earthquake in flamboyant Sicilian Baroque, the Val di Noto is a UNESCO cluster of golden limestone towns with ornate facades and wide piazzas.
trips.cover_search_query: Noto Baroque cathedral Sicily Italy

trips.name: Western Sicily & Temple Valley
trips.description: Western Sicily blends Greek, Arab, and Norman history with dramatic salt pans, ancient temples, and one of the best tuna dishes on earth. Agrigento's Valley of the Temples is unmissable.
trips.cover_search_query: Valley of Temples Agrigento Sicily Italy

trips.name: Taormina & Mount Etna
trips.description: Taormina's clifftop Greek-Roman theatre frames Europe's largest active volcano. Drive the Etna road circuit through lava fields, pistachio orchards, and wine estates at altitude.
trips.cover_search_query: Mount Etna volcano Taormina Sicily Italy

trips.name: Palermo & the Conca d'Oro
trips.description: Palermo is one of the world's great street-food cities — arancini, panelle, and sfincione at every corner. The Norman cathedrals of the Conca d'Oro valley are equally extraordinary.
trips.cover_search_query: Cefalù Norman cathedral Sicily Italy

trips.name: Sicily's Tyrrhenian Coast
trips.description: Sicily's northern coastline swoops between Greek ruins, medieval hilltowns, and clear Tyrrhenian waters. Tindari's clifftop sanctuary and the islands of the Aeolian archipelago draw the eye.
trips.cover_search_query: Tindari cliff sanctuary sea Sicily Italy

trips.name: Costa Smeralda & Gallura
trips.description: The Costa Smeralda's turquoise coves rival the Caribbean. Beyond the jet-set resorts of Porto Cervo, granite-sculpted Gallura offers wild hiking, local Vermentino wine, and nuraghe stone towers.
trips.cover_search_query: Costa Smeralda turquoise sea Sardinia Italy

trips.name: Southern Sardinia & Cagliari
trips.description: Cagliari's Phoenician-Roman port opens into a south Sardinia of pink flamingos, ancient nuraghe fortresses, and the spectacular white dunes of Piscinas.
trips.cover_search_query: Cagliari harbor Sardinia Italy

trips.name: Nuraghe Trail — Sardinia's Bronze Age
trips.description: Sardinia has more ancient stone towers (nuraghe) than any other place on earth — over 7,000 of them. This route connects the greatest, from Su Nuraxi di Barumini to the sacred well of Santa Cristina.
trips.cover_search_query: Nuraghe Su Nuraxi Barumini Sardinia Italy

trips.name: Salento — The Heel of Italy
trips.description: The very tip of Italy's boot drips in Baroque architecture, turquoise Adriatic waters, and the hypnotic pizzica folk music of Lecce. Whitewashed towns and sea caves make every stop unforgettable.
trips.cover_search_query: Lecce Baroque cathedral Salento Puglia Italy

trips.name: Trulli Country & Valle d'Itria
trips.description: The Valle d'Itria is studded with trulli — ancient dry-stone dwellings with conical roofs. Alberobello's UNESCO-listed trulli district is the showpiece, while Locorotondo and Martina Franca enchant with their whitewashed streets.
trips.cover_search_query: Alberobello trulli houses Puglia Italy

trips.name: Gargano Peninsula
trips.description: The Gargano is Puglia's wild promontory — a spur of mountains covered in the ancient Foresta Umbra, surrounded by some of Italy's clearest Adriatic waters and peppered with pilgrimage sites.
trips.cover_search_query: Vieste Gargano sea stacks Puglia Italy

trips.name: Grande Strada delle Dolomiti
trips.description: The Great Dolomites Road is one of the most beautiful drives in the world, winding between the pink-hued vertical walls of the UNESCO Dolomite peaks from Bolzano to Cortina d'Ampezzo.
trips.cover_search_query: Dolomites peaks Passo Pordoi Italy

trips.name: Val Gardena & Sella Ronda
trips.description: The Sella Ronda circuit around the Sella massif links four Dolomite valleys and countless mountain passes. In summer the ski roads transform into a scenic driving paradise with cable cars and alpine meadows.
trips.cover_search_query: Val Gardena Dolomites mountain valley Italy

trips.name: Tre Cime di Lavaredo Loop
trips.description: The three jagged towers of Tre Cime are the iconic symbol of the Dolomites. The road to Misurina lake and the toll road to the hut at the base offer views that defy description.
trips.cover_search_query: Tre Cime di Lavaredo Dolomites Italy

trips.name: Alto Adige Wine Road
trips.description: The Südtiroler Weinstraße threads through Europe's highest vineyards. Austrian culture meets Italian warmth in this border region of Gewürztraminer, medieval castles, and apple orchards.

stops.name: Caldaro sulla Strada del Vino, Italy
stops.label: Caldaro

stops.name: Termeno sulla Strada del Vino, Italy
stops.label: Termeno

trips.cover_search_query: Alto Adige vineyards Dolomites South Tyrol Italy

trips.name: Lake Como & the Larian Triangle
trips.description: Lake Como's forked silhouette is framed by snow-capped Alps and lined with Villa del Balbianello and Villa Carlotta — the most palatial of Italy's lakeside estates. George Clooney is a neighbour.
trips.cover_search_query: Lake Como Bellagio Alps Italy

trips.name: Lake Garda Grand Tour
trips.description: Italy's largest lake spans three regions and three climates. The south is Mediterranean olive groves and Bardolino wine; the north is dramatic fjord-like scenery with windsurfers and towering cliffs.
trips.cover_search_query: Lake Garda Limone cliffs Italy

trips.name: Lake Maggiore & the Borromean Islands
trips.description: Maggiore's western shore is lined with grand hotels and Borromeo family palaces. The extraordinary Borromean Islands — particularly Isola Bella's baroque gardens — are worth the whole journey.
trips.cover_search_query: Isola Bella Borromean Islands Lake Maggiore Italy

trips.name: Venice, Murano & the Lagoon
trips.description: Beyond the Grand Canal and Rialto, Venice's lagoon holds mosaic-encrusted Torcello, glass-blowing Murano, and lace-making Burano. The Brenta Riviera of Palladian villas extends inland.
trips.cover_search_query: Venice Grand Canal gondola Italy

trips.name: Veneto Wine Roads — Prosecco & Valpolicella
trips.description: The Veneto produces more DOC wine than any Italian region. Drive the Prosecco Road's UNESCO hillside vineyards, then swing south to Amarone country in the Valpolicella.
trips.cover_search_query: Prosecco hills Valdobbiadene Veneto Italy

trips.name: Belluno Dolomites & Cadore
trips.description: The Belluno province sits at the foot of the wildest Dolomites — Titian's homeland, where the Piave river carved its valley and medieval villages cling to forested slopes.
trips.cover_search_query: Belluno Dolomites valley Italy

trips.name: Cinque Terre & Ligurian Riviera
trips.description: Five fishing villages cling to near-vertical cliffs between La Spezia and Levanto, connected by the famous Sentiero Azzurro coastal path. The striped pastel houses spilling into the sea are utterly unique.
trips.cover_search_query: Cinque Terre Vernazza village cliffs Italy

trips.name: Portofino & the Riviera di Levante
trips.description: Portofino is Italy's most glamorous fishing village, its coloured buildings mirrored in a perfectly round harbour. The Riviera di Levante runs east through Camogli and the Tigullio gulf.
trips.cover_search_query: Portofino harbour Liguria Italy

trips.name: Umbria — Mystic Heart of Italy
trips.description: Umbria calls itself Italy's green heart. Pilgrims and artists have converged here for centuries: Assisi for St Francis, Perugia for chocolate and art, Orvieto for its Gothic cathedral on a tufa plateau.
trips.cover_search_query: Assisi basilica hilltown Umbria Italy

trips.name: Valnerina & the Sibillini Foothills
trips.description: The Nera river valley cuts through the remotest Umbria, connecting waterfalls, Benedictine abbeys, and the reconstructed hilltop of Cascia. Few tourist buses reach here.
trips.cover_search_query: Cascata delle Marmore waterfall Umbria Italy

trips.name: Marche — Renaissance & Adriatic
trips.description: Urbino's Ducal Palace is one of Italy's most perfect Renaissance buildings, sitting on twin hilltops above soft green hills. The Sibillini mountains and the Conero Riviera complete this underrated region.
trips.cover_search_query: Urbino ducal palace Renaissance Marche Italy

trips.name: Emilia-Romagna Food Valley
trips.description: The stretch from Parma to Bologna is arguably the richest culinary landscape on earth — Parmigiano-Reggiano, Prosciutto di Parma, Aceto Balsamico, tortellini, and mortadella all originate here.
trips.cover_search_query: Bologna porticoes Emilia-Romagna Italy

trips.name: Motor Valley — Ferrari, Lamborghini, Maserati
trips.description: Emilia-Romagna is Italy's motor valley — birthplace of Ferrari, Lamborghini, Maserati, and Ducati. Drive between their museums on roads that double as test tracks.
trips.cover_search_query: Ferrari Museum Maranello Emilia-Romagna Italy

trips.name: Ravenna Mosaics & Adriatic Riviera
trips.description: Ravenna's UNESCO Byzantine mosaics are a miracle of colour after the fall of Rome. The Po Delta nature reserve and Dante's tomb add to an itinerary that defies expectation.
trips.cover_search_query: Ravenna Byzantine mosaics UNESCO Italy

trips.name: Langhe Wine Country
trips.description: The UNESCO Langhe hills of Barolo and Barbaresco are Italy's answer to Burgundy — rolling vineyards, castle wineries, and truffle hunting beneath hazel trees in October.
trips.cover_search_query: Barolo vineyards Langhe Piedmont Italy

trips.name: Aosta Valley — Alps & Roman Ruins
trips.description: The Aosta Valley is a Roman outpost perfectly preserved — triumphal arches, a theatre, and a forum all in a mountain city at the foot of Mont Blanc and the Matterhorn.
trips.cover_search_query: Aosta Valley Alps Mont Blanc Italy

trips.name: Rome & the Castelli Romani
trips.description: Rome needs a week alone, but the Castelli Romani volcanic hills within an hour of the city offer papal summer palaces, wine cellars, and crater lakes. Tivoli's Villa d'Este is a UNESCO garden masterpiece.
trips.cover_search_query: Rome Colosseum Forum Italy

trips.name: Etruscan Lazio — Viterbo & the North
trips.description: Northern Lazio was Etruscan heartland. Tarquinia's painted tombs are among the greatest artistic treasures of antiquity, while Viterbo's medieval quarter and volcanic Lake Bolsena reward slow exploration.
trips.cover_search_query: Civita di Bagnoregio dying city Lazio Italy

trips.name: Gran Sasso & the Abruzzo Highlands
trips.description: The Gran Sasso d'Italia is the highest peak in the Apennines, rising above an upland plateau where Marsican bears and wolves still roam. The medieval hilltowns here are Italy's least visited and most authentic.
trips.cover_search_query: Gran Sasso mountain plateau Abruzzo Italy

trips.name: Matera & Basilicata
trips.description: Matera's sassi — cave dwellings cut into a ravine — are one of Italy's most extraordinary sights, occupied continuously for 9,000 years and now a UNESCO World Heritage Site and European Capital of Culture.
highlights: Carlo Levi's Cristo si è fermato
trips.cover_search_query: Matera sassi cave city UNESCO Italy

trips.name: Calabria — Tropea & the Tyrrhenian
trips.description: Tropea perches dramatically on a clifftop above some of the clearest blue water in the Mediterranean. Calabria is Italy's most underrated region — wild, authentic, and stunningly beautiful.
trips.cover_search_query: Tropea clifftop village Calabria Italy

trips.name: Friuli & Trieste — Where Italy Meets Mitteleuropa
trips.description: Trieste is Italy's most Central European city — Habsburg coffee houses, a castle on a sea promontory, and the border with Slovenia just minutes away. The Friuli wine hills produce Friulano and Collio whites.
trips.cover_search_query: Trieste Miramare Castle seafront Italy

trips.name: Bergamo, Brescia & Franciacorta
trips.description: Bergamo's Città Alta is a medieval hilltop encircled by Venetian walls, while Brescia hides Roman ruins beneath its streets. Between them, Franciacorta's sparkling wine estates rival Champagne.
trips.cover_search_query: Bergamo alta città medieval walls Italy

trips.name: Valtellina Alpine Valley
trips.description: The Valtellina runs east from Lake Como beneath the Rhaetian Alps, producing Nebbiolo grapes on vertiginous terraced vineyards. Bormio's thermal spas and the Stelvio Pass are legendary.
trips.cover_search_query: Stelvio Pass mountain hairpins Alps Italy

trips.name: Dolomiti di Brenta & Adamello
trips.description: The Brenta Dolomites are the most spectacular outlier of the Dolomite chain — isolated towers of white rock rising above larch forests west of Trento. The Via delle Bocchette via ferrata is world-famous.
trips.cover_search_query: Brenta Dolomites towers Madonna di Campiglio Italy

trips.name: Aeolian Islands Gateway
trips.description: The Aeolian Islands are seven volcanic specks north of Sicily — Stromboli erupting nightly, Lipari's obsidian cliffs, Panarea's white terraces. Milazzo is the ferry hub for the archipelago.
trips.cover_search_query: Aeolian Islands Stromboli volcano Sicily Italy

trips.name: Agrigento & the Scala dei Turchi
trips.description: The Valley of the Temples at Agrigento rivals Athens for Greek antiquity. Nearby, the Scala dei Turchi — a brilliant white marlstone cliff sloping into the sea — is one of Italy's strangest natural wonders.
trips.cover_search_query: Scala dei Turchi white cliffs Sicily Italy

trips.name: Alghero & the Coral Riviera
trips.description: Alghero is the only Catalan-speaking city in Italy, its Spanish Gothic cathedral rising above a harbour full of lobster boats. The Coral Riviera and Neptune's Grotto sea caves extend north to Castelsardo.
trips.cover_search_query: Alghero Sardinia harbor Catalonian Italy

trips.name: Ogliastra — The Wild East of Sardinia
trips.description: Ogliastra is Sardinia's most mountainous region, home to the oldest-living communities in the world (Blue Zone), dramatic Tacco rock formations, and untouched gorges and sea caves.
trips.cover_search_query: Cala Gonone sea cave Sardinia Italy

trips.name: Bari & the Adriatic Coast
trips.description: Bari's old city is a maze of whitewashed alleyways where nonnas roll orecchiette by hand on their doorsteps. The Romanesque cathedral housing St Nicholas's bones anchors the castello quarter.
trips.cover_search_query: Trani cathedral sea Puglia Italy

trips.name: Polignano a Mare & Sea Caves
trips.description: Polignano a Mare's old town teeters on the edge of a limestone cliff above sea-carved grottos. The drive south to Monopoli passes cave restaurants, cliff diving spots, and crystal-clear Adriatic coves.
trips.cover_search_query: Polignano a Mare cliff Adriatic Puglia Italy

trips.name: Verona, Vicenza & Palladio Country
trips.description: Verona is Romeo and Juliet's city and home to a Roman arena still staging opera. Nearby Vicenza is Palladio's open-air museum — a UNESCO city whose villas shaped architecture worldwide.
trips.cover_search_query: Verona Roman Arena amphitheatre Italy

trips.name: Paneveggio — The Violin Forest
trips.description: The spruce trees of Paneveggio forest have supplied Stradivarius violins for 400 years. This peaceful Trentino loop winds through alpine pastures, Dolomite foothills, and the Fassa valley.
trips.cover_search_query: Paneveggio forest Trentino Dolomites Italy

trips.name: Riviera di Ponente — Flowers & Olive Oil
trips.description: The western Ligurian Riviera is Europe's flower market — Sanremo hosts both a music festival and the greatest cycling classic. Taggia's olive oil and Dolceacqua's Rossese wine are local treasures.
trips.cover_search_query: Sanremo Ligurian Riviera flowers Italy

trips.name: Lake Trasimeno & Western Umbria
trips.description: Trasimeno is Italy's fourth-largest lake, ringed by hill towns where Perugino painted his dreamy landscapes and where Hannibal won his greatest victory against Rome. The islands are reached by short ferry.
trips.cover_search_query: Lake Trasimeno Umbria Italy

trips.name: Turin — Baroque Capital & Mountains
trips.description: Turin is Italy's most underrated city — a grand Baroque capital of wide arcaded boulevards, the Shroud of Turin, Fiat history, and the finest chocolate and vermouth in Italy.
trips.cover_search_query: Turin Mole Antonelliana Piedmont Italy

trips.name: Monferrato — Champagne of the East
trips.description: The Monferrato hills are a lesser-known Piedmontese wine country of Barbera, Grignolino, and the rare Ruché. Medieval castles crown every hilltop between Asti and Casale Monferrato.
trips.cover_search_query: Monferrato hill castles vineyards Piedmont Italy

trips.name: Po Delta & the Adriatic Lagoons
trips.description: The Po Delta is Italy's largest wetland — a labyrinth of channels, sand bars, and lagoons where flamingos, herons, and white storks congregate. Comacchio's Venice-like canals and Ravenna's mosaics nearby.
trips.cover_search_query: Comacchio canal town Po Delta Italy

trips.name: Milan & Lombardy Day Trips
trips.description: Milan is fashion and the Last Supper, but Lombardy's surrounds offer Pavia's Carthusian monastery, Cremona's violin makers, and Mantua's ducal frescoes — all within easy reach.
trips.cover_search_query: Milan Duomo cathedral Lombardy Italy

trips.name: Sila National Park & Aspromonte
trips.description: Calabria's interior is an ancient pine forest plateau — the Sila — surrounded by mountain towns where 'Ndrangheta mythology mingles with Byzantine Orthodox churches. The Aspromonte descends to Reggio's bronzes.
trips.cover_search_query: Sila National Park forest Calabria Italy

trips.name: Pistoia, Prato & the Apennine Passes
trips.description: Pistoia's silver altarpiece and Prato's Donatello pulpit are masterpieces hidden from the crowds. The Apennine passes above them lead to the chestnut forests and ski resorts of the Abetone.
trips.cover_search_query: Pistoia medieval cathedral Tuscany Italy

trips.name: Treviso, Asolo & the Marca Trevigiana
trips.description: The Marca Trevigiana has everything Venice doesn't — cycling along canals, Prosecco in hilltop villages, radicchio on every menu, and absolute quiet. Asolo is called the city of a hundred horizons.
trips.cover_search_query: Asolo hilltop views Veneto Italy

trips.name: Abruzzo Adriatic Coast & Trabocchi
trips.description: Abruzzo's coastline is dotted with trabocchi — ancient wooden fishing machines built on stilts over the sea, many now converted into extraordinary seafood restaurants.
trips.cover_search_query: Trabocchi fishing machines Abruzzo coast Italy

trips.name: Monti Sibillini & the Flowering Plain
trips.description: In June, the Piano Grande di Castelluccio blooms with an extraordinary carpet of wildflowers visible from miles away. The Sibillini mountains surround this upland plain with peaks and medieval hermitages.
trips.cover_search_query: Piano Grande Castelluccio wildflowers Sibillini Italy

trips.name: Genoa — La Superba & the Portofino Hinterland
trips.description: Genoa is a city of layers — medieval alleyways called caruggi give way to Baroque palaces on the UNESCO Strade Nuove. Columbus's birthplace and Europe's oldest maritime republic.
trips.cover_search_query: Genoa old town caruggi Liguria Italy

trips.name: Irpinia — Campania's Hidden Wine Hills
trips.description: Irpinia is Campania's mountain interior and home to its finest wines — Taurasi, Fiano di Avellino, and Greco di Tufo. Ancient Samnite villages and sulfurous springs complete the picture.
trips.cover_search_query: Irpinia wine hills Campania Italy

trips.name: Caserta Royal Palace & Terra di Lavoro
trips.description: The Reggia di Caserta is Europe's largest royal palace — 1,200 rooms, 47 staircases, and a garden cascade stretching 3km up the hill. The surrounding Terra di Lavoro is Italy's finest market garden.
trips.cover_search_query: Reggia di Caserta royal palace Italy

trips.name: Arezzo, Cortona & the Casentino
trips.description: Arezzo's Piero della Francesca frescoes are a pilgrimage for art lovers; Cortona's hillside views inspired Under the Tuscan Sun. The Casentino forest valley shelters hermit monasteries.
highlights: Francis of Assisi at La Verna
trips.cover_search_query: Cortona hilltop Tuscany Italy

trips.name: Pontine Coast & Islands
trips.description: The Pontine archipelago — Ponza and Ventotene — offers some of the clearest water in the Tyrrhenian Sea. The mainland coast has Sperlonga's cliff village and the gardens of Ninfa.
trips.cover_search_query: Ponza island Tyrrhenian Sea Lazio Italy

trips.name: Southern Tuscany — Montalcino to the Sea
trips.description: From Brunello country at Montalcino, this route descends through spa towns, thermal baths, and the stark beauty of the Crete Senesi to the Argentario coast and Giglio island.
trips.cover_search_query: Saturnia thermal waterfalls Tuscany Italy

trips.name: Friuli Wine Country — Collio & Colli Orientali
trips.description: The Collio hills on the Slovenian border produce some of Italy's greatest white wines — Friulano, Ribolla Gialla, and Pinot Grigio — from vine-covered slopes with views into two countries.
trips.cover_search_query: Collio wine hills Friuli Italy

trips.name: Alta Badia — Ladin Peaks
trips.description: Alta Badia is the heart of the Ladin-speaking Dolomites — a culture closer to Celtic-Roman than Italian, preserved in language, cuisine, and tradition. The Michelin-starred restaurants up here are extraordinary.
trips.cover_search_query: Alta Badia Dolomites Ladin valley Italy

trips.name: Euganean Hills & the Terme di Abano
trips.description: The Euganean Hills are a volcanic anomaly rising from the Venetian plain — dotted with Este dynasty castles, Arquà Petrarch's home, and the thermal spas of Abano Terme.
trips.cover_search_query: Euganean Hills Este castle Veneto Italy

trips.name: Medieval Towers — San Gimignano & Volterra
trips.description: Medieval Manhattan meets Etruscan hilltop: San Gimignano's 14 surviving towers define the Tuscan skyline while alabaster-carving Volterra perches above deep erosion gullies called balze.
trips.cover_search_query: San Gimignano medieval towers Tuscany Italy

trips.name: Sicily's Inland — Enna & the Mosaics
trips.description: Sicily's interior is a forgotten world of Greek foundations and Byzantine mosaics. Piazza Armerina's Roman villa contains the finest ancient mosaics outside Rome, hidden under a hillside for a thousand years.
trips.cover_search_query: Piazza Armerina Roman villa mosaics Sicily Italy

trips.name: Salento's Best Beaches
trips.description: The Ionian side of Salento has shallow turquoise waters and fine white sand beaches rivalling the Caribbean. Porto Cesareo and Torre dell'Orso are the gems, backed by olive and almond groves.
trips.cover_search_query: Salento turquoise sea beach Puglia Italy

trips.name: Lake Orta & Piedmont's Quiet Lake
trips.description: Lake Orta is Italy's most romantic and least visited lake — smaller than Como, quieter than Maggiore, with the island of San Giulio floating at its centre like a mirage of frescoed churches.
trips.cover_search_query: Lake Orta San Giulio island Piedmont Italy

trips.name: Riviera del Conero
trips.description: The Monte Conero promontory plunges into the Adriatic creating cliff-backed coves of crystalline water. Sirolo and Numana are the two white-walled resort villages above the best beaches.
trips.cover_search_query: Riviera del Conero cliffs Adriatic Italy

trips.name: Agordino — The Quiet Dolomites
trips.description: The Agordino valley south of Cortina is where the Dolomites were first scientifically described. Fewer crowds and more authentic mountain villages make this a rewarding contrast to the busier valleys.
trips.cover_search_query: Alleghe lake Dolomites Civetta Italy

trips.name: Naples & the Phlegraean Fields
trips.description: West of Naples, the Phlegraean Fields are a volcanic landscape of steaming craters, ancient Roman spa towns, and the entrance to Hades according to Virgil — a bizarre and utterly memorable detour.
trips.cover_search_query: Phlegraean Fields volcanic crater Naples Italy

trips.name: Spoleto & the Dukes of Umbria
trips.description: Spoleto's Rocca fortress on a mountain spur, the Romanesque cathedral, and the soaring Ponte delle Torri aqueduct make it one of Italy's most dramatic towns. The Festival dei Due Mondi draws artists worldwide.
trips.cover_search_query: Spoleto Rocca fortress Umbria Italy

trips.name: Asti & the Sparkling Wine Route
trips.description: The hills around Asti produce Italy's most famous sparkling wine — Moscato and Asti Spumante — from Moscato Bianco grapes grown on yellow limestone hills. The medieval palio horse race still runs in September.
trips.cover_search_query: Asti wine hills Moscato Piedmont Italy

trips.name: Rovereto & the Lagarina Valley
trips.description: Rovereto's MART is one of Italy's finest contemporary art museums. The Lagarina valley below the Dolomites has Marzemino wine, Roman roads, and WWI memorials — a compact and rewarding itinerary.
trips.cover_search_query: Rovereto MART museum Trentino Italy

trips.name: Rieti Valley & the Sabina Olive Country
trips.description: The Rieti valley claims to be the geographical centre of Italy. The Sabina hills above Rome are covered in ancient olive groves producing the finest DOP oil in Lazio, with Franciscan monasteries hidden among them.
trips.cover_search_query: Sabina olive groves Lazio Italy

trips.name: Emilia's Most Beautiful Villages
trips.description: The Emilian Apennines hide some of Italy's most beautiful borghi — medieval fortified villages perched above river gorges. Brisighella, Dozza, and Riolo Terme are the gems.
trips.cover_search_query: Brisighella medieval village Emilia-Romagna Italy
